arts.ttl: Crystal Palace plot £20 million move for Championship prodigy
arts.sum: Crystal Palace are keen on signing the Sunderland midfielder Chris Rigg. According to a report from the Sun, they are plotting a £20 million plus move for the 17-year-old midfielder who has impressed in the Championship with the Black Cats. Rigg is regarded as one of the finest young talents in English football right now, […] The post Crystal Palace plot £20 million move for Championship prodigy appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Own a poppy-embroidered shirt from Palace v Fulham
arts.sum: Fans have the chance to own a unique piece of club history by bidding to win a poppy-embroidered matchworn shirt from Crystal Palace's match against Fulham on Saturday afternoon (9th November 2024).

arts.ttl: Crystal Palace boss answers whether Cheick Doucoure is ready for Premier League start after injury layoff
arts.sum: Oliver Glasner admitted only players at the top of their fitness level will start for Crystal Palace in their Premier League clash against Fulham on Saturday – with Cheick Doucoure only returning to match action last weekend in the draw at Wolves. The Mali international has only recently returned from a toe injury and has made just one Premier League start under Glasner due to missing the majority of last season with an Achillies injury he picked up in the 2-1 defeat at Luton in November.

arts.ttl: Glasner on Andersen’s return to Selhurst and the Academy pathway
arts.sum: Fulham’s visit to Selhurst Park on Saturday means the return of a fan favourite in Joachim Andersen to SE25, and manager Oliver Glasner reflected on his time at the club.

arts.ttl: Eddie Nketiah injury confirmed: Crystal Palace's Oliver Glasner takes blame after denying 'suspicious' reports
arts.sum: Crystal Palace manager Oliver Glasner responds to 'suspicious' reports about the hamstring injury sustained by striker Eddie Nketiah.

arts.ttl: Depleted Palace look to continue their resurgence against a resilient Fulham
arts.sum: Crystal Palace find themselves without nine senior players – with França, Riad, Wharton, Eze, Hughes, Lerma, Ward, Richards and Nketiah all due to miss the match. A dramatic injury-time comeback win against local rivals Brentford will have elated their fanbase, and they will be looking to carry this momentum to Selhurst Park on Saturday.

arts.ttl: Academy Internationals: Palace duo on duty this November
arts.sum: Two Crystal Palace Academy prospects have received international call-ups ahead of the final international break of the year.

arts.ttl: Premier League: Predictions for all six games on Saturday
arts.sum: The Premier League reaches matchday 11 this weekend and Jack Ogalbe has collated our writers' best bets for all six games being played on Saturday

arts.ttl: Team News: Crystal Palace vs. Fulham injury, suspension list, predicted XIs
arts.sum: Sports Mole rounds up all of the latest injury and suspension news ahead of Saturday's Premier League clash between Crystal Palace and Fulham.

arts.ttl: The key quotes from Glasner's pre-Fulham press conference
arts.sum: Oliver Glasner says rediscovering the basics of Crystal Palace's desired playing style as a team has been key to their recent resurgence in results.
